context: Per premortem dab0766 A2/A3 amendment: line scanner emits ZERO findings on ALL 4 RED fixtures (string-literal guard at vuln.rs:603-604 traps `.arg("-c").arg(&cmd)`; other 3 fixtures lack any line-scanner trigger). Therefore dedupe predicate cannot be empirically verified against the test corpus — must be verified via 3 constructed synthetic fixtures (NOT added to the test corpus).
predicate: line_findings.retain(|line_f| match line_f.vuln_type { SqlInjection | CommandInjection => !canonical.iter().any(|c| c.vuln_type == line_f.vuln_type && c.line == line_f.line), _ => true })

fixtures.source: pub fn handler() { let cmd = std::env::var("CMD").unwrap(); std::process::Command::new("sh").arg(&cmd).status().unwrap(); }
fixtures.source_layout: Command::new("sh").arg(&cmd) on ONE line WITHOUT preceding `.arg("...")` — line scanner CommandInjection emits because string-literal guard at vuln.rs:603-604 doesn't trip; canonical CommandInjection emits because env_var → Command::new sink edge exists
fixtures.expected: dedupe collapses line-scanner CommandInjection on that line (canonical wins)

observed_findings.title: Potential Panic From unwrap()
observed_findings.source: line_scanner (smell-only, NOT deduped — correct per predicate)

fixtures.verdict: DEDUPE WORKS — 0 line-scanner CommandInjection findings remain at line 6 (would have emitted as title='Unsanitized Process Argument' but doesn't appear). The 2 visible CommandInjection findings are BOTH canonical (multi-source amplification at canonical layer; not a dedupe bug). Predicate correctly dropped the line-scanner CommandInjection at line 6.
fixtures.is_path_outside_test_dir: YES (/tmp/...) so is_rust_test_file returns false → Panic emissions enabled — confirms premortem E3 expectation

fixtures.source: pub fn handler() { let cmd = std::env::var("CMD").unwrap(); let mut c = std::process::Command::new("sh"); /*blank*/ c.arg(&cmd).status().unwrap(); }
fixtures.source_layout: Command::new("sh") on line 7 (ends with ;), .arg(&cmd) on line 10
fixtures.expected_dedupe_behavior: If both layers emit at line 10 → collapse to 1; if line keys diverge → 2 findings or 0 if neither emits
fixtures.verdict: OBSERVATIONAL — Neither layer emits CommandInjection. Canonical: tldr taint reports sources=2, sinks=1, flows=0 (multi-line def-use chain doesn't link in this construction). Line scanner: in_command_block is reset by the trailing ';' on line 7 before reaching line 10's .arg(&cmd), so line scanner emits 0 CommandInjection on line 10. Multi-line chain edge case: BOTH layers are silent here; dedupe-predicate has no work to do. Acknowledged limitation: line keys cannot diverge if neither layer emits. Surface as a known limitation; do NOT widen dedupe predicate within this milestone (atomic-commit boundary preserved).

fixtures.source: pub fn handler() { let cmd = std::env::var("CMD").unwrap(); unsafe { std::process::Command::new("sh").arg(&cmd).status().unwrap(); } }
fixtures.source_layout: unsafe block AND Command::new(...).arg(&cmd) AND .unwrap() on the same line 6
fixtures.expected: dedupe predicate filters by vuln_type equality → keeps line-scanner UnsafeCode AND canonical CommandInjection separately

observed_findings.title: Unsafe Block Without Safety Rationale
observed_findings.source: line_scanner (NOT DEDUPED — different vuln_type from canonical's CommandInjection on same line)

fixtures.verdict: DEDUPE PREDICATE CORRECT — UnsafeCode (line scanner) preserved alongside CommandInjection (canonical) on the same line 6. The predicate's vuln_type-equality filter keeps cross-type findings separate. Premortem stress-test case 'line-scanner UnsafeCode on line 5 + canonical CommandInjection on line 5' empirically confirmed.

summary.fixture_1_dedupe_collapse: WORKS — line-scanner CommandInjection at line 6 dropped; canonical preserved
summary.fixture_2_multi_line_chain: NEITHER LAYER EMITS — line-key divergence is moot when both layers are silent; documented limitation
summary.fixture_3_cross_vuln_preservation: WORKS — UnsafeCode + CommandInjection on same line both preserved (different vuln_type → predicate keeps both)
summary.predicate_correctness: VERIFIED on the 3 cases that the premortem stress-tested. Limitation: dedupe is line-precision-scoped; if canonical and line-scanner emit at slightly different lines (line vs. line+N), no collapse occurs (acceptable — separate sites are legitimately distinct findings).
